description: Per-domain cache TTL, rate limits, source tier, and Terms-of-Service notes. Loops 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, and 9 consult this before every fetch. A cache hit (now < expires_at and fetch_status ok) skips the fetch and consumes 0 search budget. TTLs are tuned to signal volatility: social/community activity short, directories/associations long.

youtube.com.notes: Use public channel/about pages for subscriber counts. Use YouTube Data API where available. Never scrape behind login.

tiktok.com.notes: Profile follower counts are public; engagement often estimated. Cross-check with Social Blade. Mark estimates Unverified.

instagram.com.notes: Login-gated for most data; use public profile counts + search snippets. Mark estimates Unverified.

x.com.notes: Follower counts public on profile; engagement via snippets. Formerly twitter.com.

linkedin.com.notes: Heavily login-gated. Use public company/creator pages + search snippets only. Respect robots.

socialblade.com.notes: Third-party estimator for cross-checking follower/subscriber growth. Estimates, not authoritative.

reddit.com.notes: Use the public JSON API (.json suffix). Respect Reddit's 60 rpm. Read each subreddit's rules for self-promo policy.

disboard.org.notes: Discord server directory with member counts + tags.

facebook.com.notes: Groups largely login-gated; use public about pages + search snippets. Mark member counts Unverified if not public.

indiehackers.com.notes: Founder/operator community; good for B2B SaaS ICP.

clutch.co.notes: Agency directory with verified reviews, pricing band, and client logos.

designrush.com.notes: Agency directory. Some listings are pay-to-play; cross-check before trusting ranking.

g2.com.notes: Software reviews; great for ICP language + competitor set.

passionfroot.me.notes: Creator/newsletter sponsorship marketplace with REAL priced inventory + availability.

paved.com.notes: Newsletter sponsorship marketplace with subscriber counts + CPMs.

beehiiv.com.notes: Newsletter platform; many publish subscriber counts + sponsor pages.

substack.com.notes: Newsletter platform; subscriber counts sometimes public.

listennotes.com.notes: Podcast database with listen-score ranking + contact.

meetup.com.notes: Local + virtual events with attendee/RSVP counts.

crunchbase.com.notes: Most data behind login. Use only free-tier fields + search snippets for funding signals.

similarweb.com.notes: Traffic-source estimates for competitors. Estimates, not authoritative — mark as such.

paywall_fallback_chain: search the headline / handle for a syndicated / open mirror
use headline + public snippet only, flag [unverified — public snippet only]; never fabricate counts, contacts, or rates
global_rules.anti_hallucination: If a follower/subscriber/member count, contact, price, or availability cannot be retrieved from a verifiable source, mark it Unverified. NEVER estimate or invent it.
